title: S/R level computation + session-start freshness guard
acceptance_criteria: Pure, unit-testable functions to compute objective S/R levels for a given underlying + as-of date from TimescaleDB hypertable history (time-filtered queries only — never a full hypertable scan): previous-week High/Low, monthly classic pivot ((H+L+C)/3) with R1/S1/R2/S2, and Volume Point of Control (POC).
POC degrades gracefully when volume is NULL/absent: it is omitted (not faked), and the function reports which level types contributed so callers can set poc_used downstream.
Each level carries a strength score combining proximity, confluence (count of nearby levels within a band), and a volume weight (neutral weight when volume is null — R-I consistency).

Session-start freshness/coverage guard (R-D): a function asserts historical coverage per underlying meets an expected-bars threshold for the lookback window; below threshold it THROWS (throw-don't-skip, mirroring FROZEN_VIOLATION) so the caller disables S/R for that index that day rather than silently emitting against stale/zero levels.
Property/unit tests with fast-check on the pivot math, prev-week H/L windowing (IST week boundaries), POC bucketing, the null-volume degrade path, and the coverage-guard throw path.
tsc --noEmit clean; named exports only; takes an injectable Clock (never Date.now()).
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision (esp. POC bucketing, IST week/month boundary handling, and the strength-score weighting)
